workers.focus: Focus on test rigor and edge cases.
Focus on simplicity and economy of implementation.
Focus on defensive robustness: validate inputs and fail loudly on contract violations.
Focus on API ergonomics for the downstream UI task that will build on this engine.

divergence: file footprints identical (metric blind to content; foci forced high content divergence, 1.4k-2k diff lines pairwise, test counts 36-84); no round-2 trigger

review.seed_tip_audit: 0 violated; 7/9 universally honored; 1 partially (empty-pool raise present in all source, no suite forces the branch); 1 N/A this round (argparse)

verdict.reasoning: All five pass the mechanical gate, but a reviewer probe found one real defect: Candidate A's is_lost(state) returns remaining_water <= 0 without the 'and not is_won(state)' guard, so a fully-revealed word at zero water is misclassified as a loss (is_won and is_lost both True). A's bug is masked inside apply_guess (win is checked first) and A's own suite never calls is_lost on a won state, which is exactly why it slipped the gate. B, C, D, and E all guard is_lost correctly and all 31 probe assertions pass for each. Among the four correct candidates, D wins: the most rigorous and readable suite (80 tests) covering precisely the edges that matter, including the very test A lacked (is_lost false when won, win-on-last-water precedence), clean immutable transitions, exact-string message assertions against imported constants, and spec-faithful win/loss text. Runner-up flaws: E drops SPEC 6.7's 'ran out of water' sense from MSG_LOSS; C leans on substring/attribute assertions (weaker rot guard) and skips .upper() in new_game; B is leanest and best-factored but covers the fewest edges. A is disqualified by the is_lost defect.

review.progression: Baseline: the seed commit (spec, task briefs, fleet config, 9-entry tips seed -- no code). This merge creates the project's entire executable surface: a pure, headless engine (word_garden/game.py + words.py) implementing the full rule set -- difficulty-filtered word selection with injectable randomness, stateless input validation, guess application with win-before-loss precedence and terminal status messages, masked-word display, standalone is_won/is_lost predicates -- plus an 80-test suite. Nothing is playable yet by design (zero I/O); the engine's signatures and MSG_* constants are published as the frozen contract task-002 builds on. Decisive margin over the field: the adopted candidate is the one whose suite directly tests the win-on-last-water precedence edge that disqualified candidate A.
